insights:
The single best deal among current models is DeepSeek V4 Flash. It delivers an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, which is solid for most production tasks, at a blended price of just $0.06 per million tokens. This yields a value score of 666.7, by far the highest in the benchmark. It also offers 120 tokens per second throughput and a full 1000K context window, making it both fast and capable for long documents or heavy reasoning. No other model matches this combination of cost, capability, and speed.

Two notable runners-up present clear trade-offs. MiMo-V2.5 is identical in price at $0.06/1M but scores lower on intelligence (37/60) and is roughly half as fast at 63 t/s, making it a secondary choice if DeepSeek V4 Flash is unavailable. For applications that need higher intelligence, MiniMax M3 scores 44/60 at $0.22/1M, giving a value score of 200. It is slower at 96 t/s but still fast enough for many uses, and supports 1000K context. Another option is Step 3.7 Flash, which is blindingly fast at 396 t/s and costs $0.18/1M, but its intelligence of only 30/60 limits its usefulness for complex reasoning tasks.

For the most common cost‑sensitive API use—where you need reliable performance without overspending—start with DeepSeek V4 Flash. It offers the best ratio of intelligence to price, adequate speed, and ample context. If your workload requires higher reasoning capability and you can accept a moderate cost increase, upgrade to MiniMax M3. Avoid models like Ling‑2.6‑flash; despite identical pricing, their low intelligence makes them poor value for any non‑trivial task. This combination gives you both the lowest cost and the ability to scale quality when needed.
